document.title: Usability Engineering Report for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering report for H&abyz B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents the usability engineering process, use specification, hazards, hazard-related use scenarios, user interface requirements, verification, and summative evaluation references for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
keyPoints: The report applies to Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Applicable standards are listed as EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010.
The usability process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors under normal use.
All identified hazard-related use scenarios were selected for summative evaluation.
Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-08 are recorded as Pass.

evidence.claim: The application is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with model names B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Application: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Model name: B1417MCW / B1717MCW / FB1417MCW / FB1717MCW)
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document identifies EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 as applicable standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicable standards: EN 62366-1:2015 and EN 60601-1-6:2010

evidence.claim: The normative references include IEC TR 62366-2:2016 and EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC TR 62366-2:2016 Medical devices — Part 2: Guidance on the application of usability engineering to medical devices EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: is indicated as a digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adults and children
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Use

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnoses for physicians and radiologists.
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Use

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The intended patient population includes adults and children except infants, and excludes pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Age group Adult and Children (except infants) Patient group/state except pregnant women

evidence.claim: The intended user profile includes radiologists.
evidence.evidenceText: Experience Radiologist

evidence.claim: The use environment is an X-ray room with specified operating temperature, humidity, and air pressure conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Place X-ray room Operation condition: 10℃ to 40℃ Operation condition: 20 to 75% relative humidity (non-condensing) Operation condition: 700 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process addresses risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors in normal use.
evidence.evidenceText: This usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors, i.e., normal use.

evidence.claim: The process can identify but does not assess or mitigate risks associated with abnormal use.
evidence.evidenceText: it can be used to identify but does not assess or mitigate risks associated with abnormal use.

evidence.claim: Primary operating functions include connecting power, cables, powering on the detector, patient positioning, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Connecting the power cord to the AC/DC Adapter Connecting the AC/DC Adapter and LAN Cable to the gender cable LAN cable and PC main body connection Connecting the Gender Cable to the Detector Power on the Detector Arrange the patient in the correct position to have Detector aligned with the target body part Calibration of Detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: Foreseeable harms include delayed diagnosis, misdiagnosis, electric shock, and burn.
evidence.evidenceText: Delayed diagnosis of patients misdiagnosis Electric shock burn

evidence.claim: All identified hazard-related use scenarios were used for summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All usage scenarios we identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report A-TCF-10-107.
evidence.evidenceText: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-10-107).

evidence.claim: Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-08 are Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Verification Result UI-01 No issues Pass UI-02 No issues Pass UI-03 No issues Pass UI-04 No issues Pass UI-05 No issues Pass UI-06 No issues Pass UI-07 No issues Pass UI-08 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: User Interface of Unknown Provenance is not applicable to the device.
evidence.evidenceText: However, this clause is not applicable on our device, Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW).
